  <script>
   if (document.location.protocol !== "https:") {
  document.location = document.URL.replace(/^http:/i, "https:");
}

let url = new URL(window.location.href);

if (url.href !== url.href.toLowerCase()) {
    let lowerCaseUrl = url.href.toLowerCase();
    let newUrl = new URL(lowerCaseUrl);
    window.location.href = newUrl.href;
}
  </script>

  <script type="text/javascript">
   jQuery(function () {

		var mobileOnly = false;
		
		if (mobileOnly) {
			return;
		}

		jQuery.fn.getTitle = function () { // Copy the title of every IMG tag and add it to its parent A so that fancybox can show titles
			var arr = jQuery("a[data-fancybox]");jQuery.each(arr, function() {var title = jQuery(this).children("img").attr("title") || '';var figCaptionHtml = jQuery(this).next("figcaption").html() || '';var processedCaption = figCaptionHtml;if (figCaptionHtml.length && typeof DOMPurify === 'function') {processedCaption = DOMPurify.sanitize(figCaptionHtml, {USE_PROFILES: {html: true}});} else if (figCaptionHtml.length) {processedCaption = jQuery("<div>").text(figCaptionHtml).html();}var newTitle = title;if (processedCaption.length) {newTitle = title.length ? title + " " + processedCaption : processedCaption;}if (newTitle.length) {jQuery(this).attr("title", newTitle);}});		}

		// Supported file extensions

				var thumbnails = jQuery("a:has(img)").not(".nolightbox").not('.envira-gallery-link').not('.ngg-simplelightbox').filter(function () {
			return /\.(jpe?g|png|gif|mp4|webp|bmp)(\?[^/]*)*$/i.test(jQuery(this).attr('href'))
		});
		

		// Add data-type iframe for links that are not images or videos.
		var iframeLinks = jQuery('.fancyboxforwp').filter(function () {
			return !/\.(jpe?g|png|gif|mp4|webp|bmp|pdf)(\?[^/]*)*$/i.test(jQuery(this).attr('href'))
		}).filter(function () {
			return !/vimeo|youtube/i.test(jQuery(this).attr('href'))
		});
		iframeLinks.attr({"data-type": "iframe"}).getTitle();

				/* Custom Expression */
		window.addEventListener('load', function () {
  (function($) {
    $(function() {
		if($("a[href$='.jpg'] , a[href$='.png']").hasClass("nofancybox"))
        {					
	    $("a[href$='.jpg'] , a[href$='.png']").attr("target","_blank");
		}
		else
		{
      $("a[href$='.jpg'] , a[href$='.png']").addClass("fancybox").attr("data-fancybox","cl-group").fancyboxforwp({
        buttons: [
          "zoom",
          "share",
          "slideShow",
          "fullScreen",
          "download",
          "close"
        ]
      });
	
	}
    });
  })(jQuery);
})

           <div class="elementor-column elementor-col-50 elementor-inner-column elementor-element elementor-element-323754b elementor-hidden-mobile" data-element_type="column" data-id="323754b" id="nav-search-box-container">
            <div class="elementor-widget-wrap elementor-element-populated">
             <div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4505c1e elementor-widget__width-auto elementor-widget elementor-widget-html" data-element_type="widget" data-id="4505c1e" data-widget_type="html.default" id="search-html-container">
              <div class="elementor-widget-container">
               <form action="/" aria-label="Search form" class="" id="formtoseach" role="search">
                <div class="input-group">
                 <input aria-label="Search" class="form-control nav-search-input" id="nav-search-input" name="s" placeholder="Search" style="
					display:none;" type="text" value=""/>
                 <span class="input-group-append">
                  <button aria-label="Search button" class="btn text-white" id="search-nav" title="Search" type="submit">
                   <i class="fa fa-search" style="line-height: 20px;">
                   </i>
                  </button>
                  <button aria-label="Close search" class="nav-search-close" type="button">
                   ✕
                  </button>
                 </span>
                </div>
               </form>
               <script>
                jQuery(function ($) {

  const $header = $('.header-sa-nav');
  const $searchBtn = $('#search-nav');
  const $input = $('.nav-search-input');
  const $searchContainer = $('#search-html-container');

  // Add close button once
  if (!$('.nav-search-close').length) {
    $searchBtn.after(
      '<button type="button" class="nav-search-close" aria-label="Close search">✕</button>'
    );
  }

  const $closeBtn = $('.nav-search-close');

  // OPEN
  $searchBtn.on('click', function (e) {
      
      $("#search-nav").addClass("btn-secondary");
      $("#search-nav").addClass("btn-secondary-radio-border");
            
    if (window.innerWidth <= 1190 && window.innerWidth >= 801) {
      e.preventDefault();
      $header.addClass('header-search-active');
      $searchContainer.css('width','90%');      $input.focus();
      $closeBtn
      .css({
        opacity: 1,
        pointerEvents: 'unset'
      });
    }
  });

  // CLOSE
  $closeBtn.on('click', function () {
      
     $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-secondary");
     $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-secondary-radio-border");
    $header.removeClass('header-search-active');
    $searchContainer.css('width','auto');  
    $input.val('').hide();
  });
  
  
  
   $("#search-nav").on("mouseenter", function(event ){
  
          $("#search-nav").addClass("btn-search-hover");

    });
    
    $("#search-nav").on("mouseleave", function(event ){
  
          $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-search-hover");

    });
    
    
     let lastWidth = window.innerWidth;

  $(window).on('resize', function () {
    const currentWidth = window.innerWidth;

    // Only react if breakpoint range changes
    const isInRange  = currentWidth <= 1190 && currentWidth >= 801;

    if (isInRange) {
      resetHeaderSearch();
    }

    lastWidth = currentWidth;
  });

  function resetHeaderSearch() {
    const $header = $('.header-sa-nav');

    $header.removeClass('header-search-active');

    $('.nav-search-input')
      .val('')
      .hide();

    $('.nav-search-close')
      .css({
        opacity: 0,
        pointerEvents: 'none'
      });

    $('#top-menu-desktop')
      .css({
        opacity: 1,
        pointerEvents: 'auto'
      });
      
     $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-search-hover");
     
     $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-secondary");
     
     $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-secondary-radio-border");
     
     $searchContainer.css('width','auto'); 
     
  }

  
  
  
});

    
    /*
      $("#search-nav").on("click", function(event ){
  
          $("#search-nav").addClass("btn-secondary");
            $("#search-nav").addClass("btn-secondary-radio-border");
           $(".nav-search-input").css("visibility", "visible");
    });
    
     $("#search-nav").on("mouseenter", function(event ){
  
          $("#search-nav").addClass("btn-search-hover");

    });
    
    $("#search-nav").on("mouseleave", function(event ){
  
          $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-search-hover");

    });
    */
               </script>
              </div>

     <section class="elementor-section elementor-top-section elementor-element elementor-element-07dc327 elementor-section-full_width elementor-hidden-mobile elementor-hidden-tablet elementor-section-height-default elementor-section-height-default elementor-sticky elementor-sticky__spacer" data-element_type="section" data-id="07dc327" data-particle-mobile-disabled="false" data-particle_enable="false" data-settings='{"sticky":"top","background_background":"classic","sticky_on":["desktop"],"sticky_offset":0,"sticky_effects_offset":0,"sticky_anchor_link_offset":0}' style="visibility: hidden; transition: none; animation: auto ease 0s 1 normal none running none;">
      <div class="elementor-container elementor-column-gap-no">
       <div class="elementor-column elementor-col-100 elementor-top-column elementor-element elementor-element-55efb8a" data-element_type="column" data-id="55efb8a">
        <div class="elementor-widget-wrap elementor-element-populated">
         <section class="elementor-section elementor-inner-section elementor-element elementor-element-8c1c01b elementor-section-full_width elementor-section-content-middle elementor-section-height-min-height header-sa-nav elementor-section-height-default" data-element_type="section" data-id="8c1c01b" data-particle-mobile-disabled="false" data-particle_enable="false" data-settings='{"background_background":"classic"}'>
          <div class="elementor-container elementor-column-gap-default">
           <div class="elementor-column elementor-col-50 elementor-inner-column elementor-element elementor-element-f11bf85" data-element_type="column" data-id="f11bf85" id="nav-site-logo-container">
            <div class="elementor-widget-wrap elementor-element-populated">
             <div class="elementor-element elementor-element-eb72632 elementor-hidden-mobile elementor-widget__width-auto elementor-widget elementor-widget-image" data-element_type="widget" data-id="eb72632" data-widget_type="image.default" id="header-sa-main-logo-desktop">
              <div class="elementor-widget-container">

           <div class="elementor-column elementor-col-50 elementor-inner-column elementor-element elementor-element-323754b elementor-hidden-mobile" data-element_type="column" data-id="323754b" id="nav-search-box-container">
            <div class="elementor-widget-wrap elementor-element-populated">
             <div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4505c1e elementor-widget__width-auto elementor-widget elementor-widget-html" data-element_type="widget" data-id="4505c1e" data-widget_type="html.default" id="search-html-container">
              <div class="elementor-widget-container">
               <form action="/" aria-label="Search form" class="" id="formtoseach" role="search">
                <div class="input-group">
                 <input aria-label="Search" class="form-control nav-search-input" id="nav-search-input" name="s" placeholder="Search" style="
					display:none;" type="text" value=""/>
                 <span class="input-group-append">
                  <button aria-label="Search button" class="btn text-white" id="search-nav" title="Search" type="submit">
                   <i class="fa fa-search" style="line-height: 20px;">
                   </i>
                  </button>
                  <button aria-label="Close search" class="nav-search-close" type="button">
                   ✕
                  </button>
                 </span>
                </div>
               </form>
               <script>
                jQuery(function ($) {

  const $header = $('.header-sa-nav');
  const $searchBtn = $('#search-nav');
  const $input = $('.nav-search-input');
  const $searchContainer = $('#search-html-container');

  // Add close button once
  if (!$('.nav-search-close').length) {
    $searchBtn.after(
      '<button type="button" class="nav-search-close" aria-label="Close search">✕</button>'
    );
  }

  const $closeBtn = $('.nav-search-close');

  // OPEN
  $searchBtn.on('click', function (e) {
      
      $("#search-nav").addClass("btn-secondary");
      $("#search-nav").addClass("btn-secondary-radio-border");
            
    if (window.innerWidth <= 1190 && window.innerWidth >= 801) {
      e.preventDefault();
      $header.addClass('header-search-active');
      $searchContainer.css('width','90%');      $input.focus();
      $closeBtn
      .css({
        opacity: 1,
        pointerEvents: 'unset'
      });
    }
  });

  // CLOSE
  $closeBtn.on('click', function () {
      
     $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-secondary");
     $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-secondary-radio-border");
    $header.removeClass('header-search-active');
    $searchContainer.css('width','auto');  
    $input.val('').hide();
  });
  
  
  
   $("#search-nav").on("mouseenter", function(event ){
  
          $("#search-nav").addClass("btn-search-hover");

    });
    
    $("#search-nav").on("mouseleave", function(event ){
  
          $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-search-hover");

    });
    
    
     let lastWidth = window.innerWidth;

  $(window).on('resize', function () {
    const currentWidth = window.innerWidth;

    // Only react if breakpoint range changes
    const isInRange  = currentWidth <= 1190 && currentWidth >= 801;

    if (isInRange) {
      resetHeaderSearch();
    }

    lastWidth = currentWidth;
  });

  function resetHeaderSearch() {
    const $header = $('.header-sa-nav');

    $header.removeClass('header-search-active');

    $('.nav-search-input')
      .val('')
      .hide();

    $('.nav-search-close')
      .css({
        opacity: 0,
        pointerEvents: 'none'
      });

    $('#top-menu-desktop')
      .css({
        opacity: 1,
        pointerEvents: 'auto'
      });
      
     $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-search-hover");
     
     $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-secondary");
     
     $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-secondary-radio-border");
     
     $searchContainer.css('width','auto'); 
     
  }

  
  
  
});

    
    /*
      $("#search-nav").on("click", function(event ){
  
          $("#search-nav").addClass("btn-secondary");
            $("#search-nav").addClass("btn-secondary-radio-border");
           $(".nav-search-input").css("visibility", "visible");
    });
    
     $("#search-nav").on("mouseenter", function(event ){
  
          $("#search-nav").addClass("btn-search-hover");

    });
    
    $("#search-nav").on("mouseleave", function(event ){
  
          $("#search-nav").removeClass("btn-search-hover");

    });
    */
               </script>
              </div>
             </div>

              <div class="elementor-widget-container">

          <div class="elementor-container elementor-column-gap-default">
           <div class="elementor-column elementor-col-100 elementor-inner-column elementor-element elementor-element-d832cdc" data-element_type="column" data-id="d832cdc">
            <div class="elementor-widget-wrap elementor-element-populated">
             <div class="elementor-element elementor-element-33cc75e elementor-widget elementor-widget-html" data-element_type="widget" data-id="33cc75e" data-widget_type="html.default">
              <div class="elementor-widget-container">
               <form action="/" aria-label="Search form" class="mobile-search-form" role="search">
                <div class="input-group">
                 <input aria-label="Search" class="form-control nav-search-input-mobile" name="s" placeholder="Search" style="border-radius: 8px 0 0 8px;" type="text" value=""/>
                 <span class="input-group-append">
                  <button aria-label="Search button" class="btn btn-secondary text-white" id="search-nav-mobile" style="border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0;" title="Search" type="submit">
                   <i class="fa fa-search" style="line-height: 20px;">
                   </i>
                  </button>
                 </span>
                </div>
               </form>
               <script>
                const $searchBtnMobile = $('#search-nav-mobile');
    
    
      $searchBtnMobile.on("click", function(e){
         e.preventDefault();
         
         $searchBtnMobile.addClass("btn-secondary");
       
        const $input = $(".nav-search-input-mobile");
        
        const value = $.trim($input.val());

        if (value !== '') {
            $input.closest("form")[0].requestSubmit();
        }
           
           
           
    });
               </script>
              </div>
             </div>
             <div class="elementor-element elementor-element-67c8231 elementor-widget elementor-widget-html" data-element_type="widget" data-id="67c8231" data-widget_type="html.default">
              <div class="elementor-widget-container">
               <script>
                //Change chevron orientation mobile menu
$("#menu-mobile-primary-nav-2023 > li > a.pp-menu-item").on("click", function(e) {
    //Do not trigger if the actual link to parent is clicked - A tag
    //Just the I or SPAN
   if("I" == e.target.nodeName || "SPAN" == e.target.nodeName) {
        let itag = $( this ).find("i");
        if($( itag ).hasClass('fa-chevron-down')) {
            $( itag ).removeClass('fa-chevron-down');
            $( itag ).addClass('fa-chevron-up');
        }
        else {
            $( itag ).removeClass('fa-chevron-up')
            $( itag ).addClass('fa-chevron-down');
        }
    } 
});

$("#menu-mobile-toggle").on("click",function(){
      if($("#menu-mobile-toggle .pp-menu-toggle").hasClass("pp-active") ){
          $("#menu-mobile-container").addClass("dropdown-menu-scrolling");
          $("#formtoseachmobile").show();
      }
      else{
          $("#menu-mobile-container").removeClass("dropdown-menu-scrolling");
          $("#formtoseachmobile").hide();
      }
      
});
               </script>
              </div>
             </div>
            </div>
           </div>
          </div>

     <div class="elementor-container elementor-column-gap-default">
      <div class="elementor-column elementor-col-50 elementor-top-column elementor-element elementor-element-f44591e" data-element_type="column" data-id="f44591e">
       <div class="elementor-widget-wrap elementor-element-populated">
        <div class="elementor-element elementor-element-aa64d98 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-element_type="widget" data-id="aa64d98" data-widget_type="heading.default">
         <div class="elementor-widget-container">
          <h3 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">
           Nature Merit Badge Overview
          </h3>
         </div>
        </div>
        <div class="elementor-element elementor-element-26d4ab6 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-element_type="widget" data-id="26d4ab6" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
         <div class="elementor-widget-container">
          There is a very close connection between the soil, the plants, and all animal life, including people. Understanding this connection, and the impact we have upon it, is important to preserving the wilderness, as well as to our own well-being as members of the web of nature.
         </div>
        </div>
       </div>
      </div>

         </div>
        </div>
        <div class="elementor-element elementor-element-4fc95a4 elementor-widget elementor-widget-heading" data-element_type="widget" data-id="4fc95a4" data-widget_type="heading.default">
         <div class="elementor-widget-container">
          <h2 class="elementor-heading-title elementor-size-default">
           The previous version of the Merit Badge requirements can be found in Scoutbook
          </h2>
         </div>
        </div>
        <div class="elementor-element elementor-element-81e1a24 elementor-hidden-desktop elementor-hidden-tablet elementor-hidden-mobile elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-element_type="widget" data-id="81e1a24" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
         <div class="elementor-widget-container">
          The requirements will be fed dynamically using the scout book integration 79
         </div>
        </div>
        <div class="elementor-element elementor-element-71db3f0 details-area elementor-widget elementor-widget-bsaIncluder" data-element_type="widget" data-id="71db3f0" data-widget_type="bsaIncluder.default">
         <div class="elementor-widget-container">
          <div class="mb-requirement-container">
           <div class="mb-requirement-item">
            <div class="mb-requirement-parent mb-requirement-id-13833">
             <span class="mb-requirement-listnumber">
             </span>
             <b>
              NOTE: In any of the collection requirements, if collecting is permitted, do so ethically and sustainably. Scouts must remember to follow the Leave No Trace Seven Principles and the Outdoor Code while on field trips and when collecting.  Collect sparingly, only taking what you need, and ensure that your collection practices do not harm the surrounding environment.  Make sure you have permission from the land manager prior to taking any samples for your collections and be aware of and adhere to local laws regarding collection. (Note that collecting is prohibited in most National and State Parks.)  Consider purchasing rock, mineral and fossil specimens from commercial rock and mineral shops or from home garden stores.  Pictures or sketches may suffice for a collection and limit impacted disturbances.
              <br/>
              In most cases all specimens should be returned to the wild at the location of original capture after the requirements have been met. Check with your counselor for those instances where the return of these specimens would not be appropriate.
              <br/>
              <br/>
              Under the Endangered Species Act of 1973, some plants and animals are or may be protected by federal law. The same ones and/or others may be protected by state law. Be sure that you do not collect protected species.
              <br/>
              <br/>
              Your state may require that you purchase and carry a license to collect certain species. Check with the wildlife and fish and game officials in your state regarding species regulations before you begin to collect.
             </b>
            </div>
           </div>
           <div class="mb-requirement-item">
            <div class="mb-requirement-parent mb-requirement-id-14132">
             <span class="mb-requirement-listnumber">
             </span>
             <b>
              NOTE:
             </b>
             The official merit badge pamphlets are now free and downloadable
             <a href="https://filestore.scouting.org/filestore/Merit_Badge_ReqandRes/Pamphlets/Nature.pdf" target="_blank">
              HERE
             </a>
             or can be purchased at the
             <a href="https://www.scoutshop.org/" target="_blank ">
              Scout Shop.
             </a>
            </div>
           </div>
           <div class="mb-requirement-item">
            <div class="mb-requirement-parent mb-requirement-id-13645">
             <span class="mb-requirement-listnumber">
              1.
             </span>
             Name three ways in which plants are important to animals. Name a plant that is protected in your state or region, and explain why it is at risk.
             <br/>
             <i>
              Resources:
             </i>
             <a href="https://www.pbs.org/video/why-animals-need-plants-y469zr" target="_blank">
              Why Animals Need Plants (video)
             </a>
             <br/>
             <a href="https://completegardening.com/11-plants-that-use-animals-to-do-their-dirty-work/" target="_blank">
              11 Plants That Use Animals to Do Their Dirty Work (website)
             </a>
            </div>
           </div>
           <div class="mb-requirement-item">
            <div class="mb-requirement-parent mb-requirement-id-13646">
             <span class="mb-requirement-listnumber">
              2.
             </span>
             Name three ways in which animals are important to plants. Name an animal that is protected in your state or region, and explain why it is at risk.
             <br/>
             <i>
              Resource:
             </i>
             <a href="https://nature-mentor.com/how-do-animals-help-plants/" target="_blank">
              How Do Animals Help Plants? (4 Symbiotic Interactions) (website)
             </a>
            </div>
           </div>
           <div class="mb-requirement-item">
            <div class="mb-requirement-parent mb-requirement-id-13647">
             <span class="mb-requirement-listnumber">
              3.
             </span>
             Explain the term "food chain." Give an example of a four-step land food chain and a four-step water food chain.
             <br/>
             <i>
              Resources:
             </i>
             <a href="https://youtu.be/JCl_yDf0Qok?si=E9r3jI_7n8iYE1Rb" target="_blank">
              Food Chains and Food Webs (video)
             </a>
             <br/>
             <a href="https://youtu.be/5Z8rKhXUYAg?si=CG1gOoYQ6n9s86LY" target="_blank">
              Food Chains in the Everglades (video)
             </a>
            </div>
           </div>
           <div class="mb-requirement-item">
            <div class="mb-requirement-parent mb-requirement-id-13617">
             <span class="mb-requirement-listnumber">
              4.
             </span>
             Do all the requirements in FIVE of the following fields:
            </div>
            <ul class="mb-requirement-children-list">
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13617 mb-requirement-id-13618">
              (a)
              <b>
               Birds.
              </b>
              Do ALL of the following:
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13618 mb-requirement-id-13619">
              (1) 
In the field, identify eight species of birds.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HtBcOk8ICpA" target="_blank">
               How to Identify Birds [TOP 10 BACKYARD BIRDS] (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://merlin.allaboutbirds.org/" target="_blank">
               Merlin Bird ID (website)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13618 mb-requirement-id-13620">
              (2) 
Make and set out a birdhouse OR a feeding station OR a birdbath. List what birds used it during a period of one month.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u4iAhnbWpz8" target="_blank">
               DIY Birdhouse (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yZl2N2XlHDA" target="_blank">
               How to Pick (or Build) the Right Bird Bath for Your Garden (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=j0werPvrrPI" target="_blank">
               Build a Bird House for Under $5 in Under 5 Minutes (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13617 mb-requirement-id-13621">
              (b)
              <b>
               Mammals.
              </b>
              Do ALL of the following:
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13621 mb-requirement-id-13622">
              (1) 
In the field, identify three species of wild mammals.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x8DfP0Nh7kA" target="_blank">
               How to Find Mammal Signs (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://www.nwf.org/Educational-Resources/Wildlife-Guide/Mammals" target="_blank">
               Mammals (website)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13621 mb-requirement-id-13623">
              (2) 
Make plaster casts of the tracks of a wild mammal.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y4WTmgo4zeA" target="_blank">
               How to Make Plaster Casts of an Animal Track (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=A_ksi4Ih_wU" target="_blank">
               How to Make Your Own Animal Track Cast (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13617 mb-requirement-id-13624">
              (c)
              <b>
               Reptiles and Amphibians.
              </b>
              Do ALL of the following:
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13624 mb-requirement-id-13625">
              (1) 
Show that you can recognize the venomous snakes in your area.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://urbanjunglewildliferemoval.com/blog/how-to-tell-venomous-snake/" target="_blank">
               How to Tell if a Snake Is Venomous (website)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://youtu.be/KxXfWrI6bB4?si=6TMzt9gUfQdxCvWc" target="_blank">
               Zoologist Explains Snake Venom - Poisonous vs Venomous (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13624 mb-requirement-id-13626">
              (2) 
In the field, identify three species of reptiles or amphibians.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/places/united-states#taxon=26036" target="_blank">
               Reptiles of the United States (website)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/places/united-states#taxon=20978" target="_blank">
               Amphibians of the United States (website)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13624 mb-requirement-id-13627">
              (3) 
Recognize one species of toad or frog by voice; OR identify one reptile or amphibian by eggs, den, burrow, or other signs.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6MsTbQi19SA" target="_blank">
               Reptiles vs. Amphibians (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://youtu.be/74-vaZv54s8?si=7ClcX_Akwhjh0A_T" target="_blank">
               Frog Sounds: Why They Make Them (Examples) (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13617 mb-requirement-id-13628">
              (d)
              <b>
               Insects and Spiders.
              </b>
              Do ALL of the following:
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13628 mb-requirement-id-13629">
              (1) 
Collect and identify either in the field or through photographs 10 species of insects or spiders.  Photos may be taken with your own equipment or gathered from other sources.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resource:
              </i>
              <a href="https://youtu.be/_Ep2ronR_io?si=VWUcH8SZodx_njzz" target="_blank">
               How to Start a Bug Collection (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13628 mb-requirement-id-13630">
              (2) 
Hatch an insect from the pupa or cocoon; OR hatch adults from nymphs; OR keep larvae until they form pupae or cocoons; OR keep a colony of ants or bees through one season.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XC_O2MN4iOk" target="_blank">
               Hatch and Raise Monarch Butterflies (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=o1421IyFKuU" target="_blank">
               How to Raise an Ant Colony 101 | The Ultimate Guide to Keeping Pet Ants (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G-nriWdyR5w" target="_blank">
               How to Raise a Caterpillar Into a Butterfly (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13617 mb-requirement-id-13631">
              (e)
              <b>
               Fish.
              </b>
              Do ALL of the following:
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13631 mb-requirement-id-13632">
              (1) 
Identify two species of fish native to your area.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resource:
              </i>
              <a href="https://igfa.org/game-fish-database/" target="_blank">
               Game Fish Identification Reference Guides (website)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13631 mb-requirement-id-13633">
              (2) 
Collect four kinds of animal food eaten by fish in the wild.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resource:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LVTGIn4gnTw" target="_blank">
               What Do Fish Eat? (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13617 mb-requirement-id-13634">
              (f)
              <b>
               Mollusks and Crustaceans.
              </b>
              Do ALL of the following:
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13634 mb-requirement-id-13635">
              (1) 
Identify five species of mollusks and crustaceans.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G_GJH1t7VVM" target="_blank">
               All About Mollusks (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://youtu.be/nfTQUX-GMjc?si=uWRRlR9ez4Tc5ymm" target="_blank">
               Crustaceans Facts (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13634 mb-requirement-id-13636">
              (2) 
Collect, mount, and label six shells.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://youtu.be/MMkynhuI_1w?si=ZC4ur2DlM9Sl2IFw" target="_blank">
               Organizing My Massive Seashell Collection. Identifying Similar Shells &amp; Comparing Shells to Critters (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://conchologistsofamerica.org/organizing-a-shell-collection/" target="_blank">
               Organizing a Shell Collection (website)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13617 mb-requirement-id-13637">
              (g)
              <b>
               Plants.
              </b>
              Do ALL of the following:
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13637 mb-requirement-id-13641">
              (1) 
In the field, identify 15 species of wild plants.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resource:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5Aj82u2he6s" target="_blank">
               How to Identify Wild Plants - A Guide To Botanical Terms (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13637 mb-requirement-id-13638">
              (2) 
Do ONE of the following:
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13638 mb-requirement-id-13639">
              (a) 
Collect and label the seeds of six plants OR the leaves of 12 plants.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=06nhKenT410" target="_blank">
               Science at Play: Preserving Fall Leaves (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QqIoGe-m2OM" target="_blank">
               Harvesting &amp; Saving Seeds! (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13638 mb-requirement-id-13640">
              (b) 
Photograph the seeds of six plants OR the leaves of 12 plants and  create a catalog of your photos.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://youtu.be/ChxvvKV2Gx8?si=PXSE1d46dKsQqDmv" target="_blank">
               Plant Photography for Beginners (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://youtu.be/gqoAL4-XTNg?si=p-r1McCO7JyXONJH" target="_blank">
               How I Shot My First Plant Time-Lapse (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13617 mb-requirement-id-13642">
              (h)
              <b>
               Soils and Rocks.
              </b>
              Do ALL of the following:
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13642 mb-requirement-id-13643">
              (1) 
Collect and identify three different types of soil that represent soils  high in sand, clay and humus.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resource:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VTuL5Pa0gC0" target="_blank">
               Layers of Soil | Soil Profile Formation (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13642 mb-requirement-id-13644">
              (2) 
Collect and identify five different types of rocks from your area.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://youtu.be/yORP1Ba-K4Q?si=iR3Lo66iW-dmFjno" target="_blank">
               Rockhounding 101: What to Look for on Your Next Adventure! (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mgld5jeYR04" target="_blank">
               Rock &amp; Mineral Identification | It Can Be Easier Than You Think (video)
              </a>
             </li>
            </ul>
            <div style="padding-left:10px; font-size:14px; color:#515354">
            </div>
            <br/>
           </div>
           <div class="mb-requirement-item">
            <div class="mb-requirement-parent mb-requirement-id-13648">
             <span class="mb-requirement-listnumber">
              5.
             </span>
             Discuss the importance of the Leave No Trace Seven Principles and the Outdoor Code and how they relate to nature. Explain how you have followed the Leave No Trace Seven Principles and the Outdoor Code while in natural areas during field observation,  specimen collection, and identification.
             <br/>
             <i>
              Resources:
             </i>
             <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XoOSgw6sOPA" target="_blank">
              The Outdoor Code Explained (video)
             </a>
             <br/>
             <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Rpq01rO9ZR0" target="_blank">
              What is Leave No Trace?  (video)
             </a>
             <br/>
             <a href="https://vimeo.com/1115216743/63b20c0b33?share=copy" target="_blank">
              Leave No Trace Basics (video)
             </a>
            </div>
           </div>
           <div class="mb-requirement-item">
            <div class="mb-requirement-parent mb-requirement-id-13649">
             <span class="mb-requirement-listnumber">
              6.
             </span>
             Do the following:
            </div>
            <ul class="mb-requirement-children-list">
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13649 mb-requirement-id-13650">
              (a) 
Explain what succession is to your counselor.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IrIvMt6HWlA" target="_blank">
               Ecological Succession  (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jZKIHe2LDP8" target="_blank">
               Ecological Succession: Change Is Good (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13649 mb-requirement-id-13651">
              (b) 
Visit a natural area (forest, grassland, meadow, water feature) and explain  what stage of succession (both plant and animal) the area is in. Talk about  what community/succession stages may have been there before and what  community/succession stages may replace what you see now. Discuss what  disturbances or changes have taken place in the past to create this landscape  and what changes may occur in the future to change the landscape further.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resource:
              </i>
              <a href="https://youtu.be/8ceDE01iWLE?si=kzFlf4WEsd2ArppS" target="_blank">
               Ecological Succession-Primary and Secondary (video)
              </a>
             </li>
            </ul>
            <div style="padding-left:10px; font-size:14px; color:#515354">
            </div>
            <br/>
           </div>
           <div class="mb-requirement-item">
            <div class="mb-requirement-parent mb-requirement-id-13652">
             <span class="mb-requirement-listnumber">
              7.
             </span>
             Do ONE of the following:
            </div>
            <ul class="mb-requirement-children-list">
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13652 mb-requirement-id-13653">
              (a) 
Identify three career opportunities that would use skills and knowledge in Nature. Pick one and research the training, education, certification requirements, experience, and expenses associated with entering the field.  Research the prospects for employment, starting salary, advancement opportunities and career goals associated with this career.  Discuss what you learned with your counselor and whether you might be interested in this career.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://youtu.be/AK2Onqx-ctM?si=OS1LiXEGCczteH1X" target="_blank">
               10+ Wildlife biology careers (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://youtu.be/GfpULQZL3tk?si=byWfIgDnVLy-xAPY" target="_blank">
               12 Environmental Career Paths for Non-Scientists (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://youtu.be/Q-3KseGxoOw?si=qn8G2GE_wnzwTI6B" target="_blank">
               10+ Outdoor &amp; Nature Job Ideas With NO Degree! (video)
              </a>
             </li>
             <li class="mb-requirement-child mb-parent-13652 mb-requirement-id-13654">
              (b) 
Identify how you might use the skills and knowledge in Nature to pursue a personal hobby.  Research the additional training required, expenses, and affiliation with organizations that would help you maximize the enjoyment and benefit you might gain from it.  Discuss what you learned with your counselor and share what short-term and long-term goals you might have if you pursued this.
              <br/>
              <i>
               Resources:
              </i>
              <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Pt1UqDNcaFg" target="_blank">
               Other Hobbies in Your Journal: The Nature Journal Show (video)
              </a>
              <br/>
              <a href="https://wildlifeinformer.com/hobbies-for-animal-and-nature-lovers/" target="_blank">
               15 Hobbies for Animal and Nature Lovers (website)
              </a>
             </li>
            </ul>
            <div style="padding-left:10px; font-size:14px; color:#515354">
            </div>
            <br/>
           </div>
          </div>
         </div>

document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function() {
    const pamphletSection =  document.querySelector('.mb-scoutshop-pamphlet');
    if(pamphletSection) {
        pamphletLink = pamphletSection.querySelector('a');

          <div class="elementor-button-wrapper">
           <a class="elementor-button elementor-button-link elementor-size-sm" href="/skills/merit-badges/all">
            <span class="elementor-button-content-wrapper">
             <span class="elementor-button-text">
              GO TO MERIT BADGES A-Z

    <div class="elementor-element elementor-element-03db9b4 e-flex e-con-boxed e-con e-parent" data-element_type="container" data-id="03db9b4" data-particle-mobile-disabled="false" data-particle_enable="false">
     <div class="e-con-inner">
      <div class="elementor-element elementor-element-a62f892 elementor-widget elementor-widget-html" data-element_type="widget" data-id="a62f892" data-widget_type="html.default">
       <div class="elementor-widget-container">
        <script>
         // Add dinamically the UTM param to the pamphlet and products
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function() {
    const shopLinks = document.querySelectorAll("a[href*='scoutshop.org']");
    
    if (shopLinks) {  
        shopLinks.forEach(link => {
            let url = new URL(link.href);
            let path = url.pathname;
    
            if (!url.searchParams.has("utm_source")) {
                
                // Confirming that the socutshop link is not a simple direct link to the ScoutShop site. 
                if(path != "/")
                {
                    url.searchParams.append("utm_source", "scouting.org");  
                    url.searchParams.append("utm_medium", "meritbadgepages");  
                    url.searchParams.append("utm_campaign", "scoutshopproducts");
                    
                    /* This CSS class is added to the link on the Pamphlet Template
                    *order to confim it is comming from that Template Section
                    */
                    if(Object.values(link.classList).includes("scoutshop-pamphlet-link")) {
                      url.searchParams.append("utm_content", "pamphlet");  
                    } else {
                      url.searchParams.append("utm_content", "product"); 
                    }
                    
                    link.href = url.toString();
                }
            }
        });
    }
});
        </script>
       </div>
      </div>
     </div>
    </div>
   </div>

  <script>
   const lazyloadRunObserver = () => {
					const lazyloadBackgrounds = document.querySelectorAll( `.e-con.e-parent:not(.e-lazyloaded)` );
					const lazyloadBackgroundObserver = new IntersectionObserver( ( entries ) => {
						entries.forEach( ( entry ) => {
							if ( entry.isIntersecting ) {
								let lazyloadBackground = entry.target;
								if( lazyloadBackground ) {
									lazyloadBackground.classList.add( 'e-lazyloaded' );
								}
								lazyloadBackgroundObserver.unobserve( entry.target );
							}
						});
					}, { rootMargin: '200px 0px 200px 0px' } );
					lazyloadBackgrounds.forEach( ( lazyloadBackground ) => {
						lazyloadBackgroundObserver.observe( lazyloadBackground );
					} );
				};
				const events = [
					'DOMContentLoaded',
					'elementor/lazyload/observe',
				];
				events.forEach( ( event ) => {
					document.addEventListener( event, lazyloadRunObserver );
				} );
  </script>

    const showMoreButton = document.querySelector('.show-more');
    const showLessButton = document.querySelector('.show-less');

    showMoreButton.addEventListener('click', function(event) {
        event.preventDefault();
        showMoreButton.classList.toggle('content-hide');
        const profileCard = showMoreButton.closest('.profile-card');
        profileCard.querySelector('.show-less').classList.toggle('content-hide');
        profileCard.querySelector('#fade').classList.toggle('content-hide');
        const requirementContainer = profileCard.querySelector('.mb-requirement-container');
        requirementContainer.classList.toggle('expanded');
    });
